notes: First-contact visibility check: after one trivial turn the dashboard shows WHICH model the session runs and HOW FULL its context is. For kiro-cli both come from the <uuid>.json metadata sidecar (session_state.rts_model_state), read by the parser at turn_done via the #599 TranscriptPathAware seam — the JSONL itself carries neither. The phase DSL asserts the state arc; the model/context assertions are evidenced by the staged-replay golden AFTER turn_end (events.jsonl carries state transitions only — no metrics frames): model_name = 'auto' (kiro's own unpinned routing label, exactly what its TUI footer shows — the acceptance admits an agent-displayed alias), context_window = 200000 (sidecar context_window_tokens, no capacity lookup), context_utilization_percentage > 0 and equal to the sidecar's context_usage_percentage (Tokens.Total is derived as pct/100 × window; raw sidecar token counts are zero — display, not accounting). Replay reproduces this only because the fixture carries transcript.json (the sidecar) next to transcript.jsonl. cost stays 0: capacity has no pricing for 'auto' (cost is not asserted by this scenario).

text: Turn completes back to ready; SessionMetrics.ModelName = 'auto' (non-empty, not 'unknown'), ContextWindow = 200000 from the sidecar, context_utilization_percentage > 0 equal to the sidecar's context_usage_percentage
